chapter_context.summary: Details Ezekiel's call and commissioning by the Spirit, who sets him on his feet and sends him to a rebellious nation of impudent and stiff-hearted children, giving him a roll of a book filled with lamentations, mourning, and woe to eat.

outline.text: Ezekiel Commissioned and Filled with the Spirit
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ezekiel Commissioned and Filled with the Spirit (2:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Command to Fear Not and the Lamentable Roll of the Book
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Command to Fear Not and the Lamentable Roll of the Book (2:6-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 2, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
